title: Fix /sales route error and implement comprehensive invoice creation system

requirements.text: Fix the error on the /sales route that displays 'Something went wrong!' and prevents access to the invoice creation page

quotes: if we click on new invoice showing like this

acceptanceCriteria: The /sales route loads without errors
Users can access the invoice creation interface
No 'Something went wrong!' error is displayed

requirements.text: Implement customer management in the invoice form with a dropdown to select from pre-registered customers stored in the backend customer database

quotes: should be from pre-registered in system

acceptanceCriteria: Invoice form displays a dropdown of all pre-registered customers
Customer dropdown shows customer name and mobile number
Selected customer's information is linked to the invoice

requirements.text: Add the ability to enter a new customer name directly in the invoice form when creating an invoice, even if the customer is not pre-registered

quotes: able to enter a new customer name directly when creating the invoice

acceptanceCriteria: Invoice form includes an option to add a new customer inline
User can input customer name without pre-registration
New customer entry is validated before invoice creation

requirements.text: Display all available recipes in the invoice form with a search and filter interface to quickly find items

quotes: able to see available recipes from and also should have search/filter interface

acceptanceCriteria: All recipes are displayed in the item selection interface
Search functionality filters recipes by name
Filter interface allows quick item lookup

requirements.text: Show recipe prices alongside recipe names in the invoice item selection interface

quotes: Should it display the recipe price alongside the name?

acceptanceCriteria: Each recipe displays its price in the selection interface
Prices are clearly visible next to recipe names
Price formatting is consistent across all recipes

requirements.text: Allow adding multiple recipe items with quantities to a single invoice

quotes: allow creating invoices for multiple items at once

acceptanceCriteria: Users can add multiple line items to one invoice
Each line item includes a recipe and quantity
Users can add, edit, and remove line items before saving
Invoice displays all added items with their quantities

requirements.text: Capture comprehensive invoice details including customer information, items with quantities, pricing, invoice date, payment method, notes, and tax calculations

quotes: invoice date, customer contact info, payment method, notes, tax calculations

acceptanceCriteria: Invoice form captures customer information
Invoice form captures all line items with quantities and prices
Invoice date is recorded (defaults to current date)
Payment method dropdown is available (cash, card, UPI, etc.)
Notes field allows additional information entry
Tax calculations are included in the invoice total
All captured data is displayed in invoice details view

requirements.text: Automatically deduct ingredient quantities from inventory based on the recipes and quantities in the invoice when the invoice is saved

quotes: When an invoice is created, should it automatically deduct ingredients from inventory based on the recipe

acceptanceCriteria: When an invoice is saved, the backend calculates ingredient requirements based on recipes and quantities
Ingredient quantities in inventory are automatically reduced
If insufficient inventory exists, display an error and prevent invoice creation
Inventory adjustments are recorded with reference to the invoice ID
Users can view inventory deduction history linked to invoices

requirements.text: Calculate and display the invoice subtotal, discount, tax, and total amount in real-time as items are added
acceptanceCriteria: Subtotal updates automatically as items are added or quantities change
Discount field allows percentage or fixed amount entry
Tax percentage is applied to calculate tax amount
Total amount reflects subtotal minus discount plus tax
All calculations are displayed clearly in the invoice form

constraints: Use existing backend actor methods for fetching customers, recipes, and ingredients
Maintain the single-actor architecture in backend/main.mo
Do not modify read-only frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
Use existing InvoiceForm.tsx component patterns and react-hook-form for form management
Ensure inventory deduction logic respects existing ingredient unit conversions (grams/kilograms)
nonGoals: Modifying existing invoice history or analytics features
Adding customer authentication or login for invoice creation
Implementing invoice editing or deletion functionality
Adding PDF export or printing features for invoices
Creating a separate customer registration page (inline addition is sufficient)
Implementing partial inventory deduction or reservation systems
